title: Reference/query pathological shift scoring
prompt: Using scPSS, load an AnnData object, define reference and query samples from metadata, optimize parameters, and compute pathological shift scores and conditions for all cells. Export a scored AnnData file and a query-cell score table.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace scPSS with a custom scoring algorithm.
Use scPSS outputs stored in AnnData obs instead of inventing new score columns.
input_spec: One .h5ad file with a sample metadata column.

artifact_checks: cell_scores.csv contains cell identifier and scPSS score columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare exported query-cell scores against a reference scPSS run on the same fixed sample split.

title: Significant pathological-cell detection
prompt: Run the scPSS workflow and identify significantly shifted query cells using the p-value and q-value outputs produced by scPSS. Export the significant-cell table and a compact distribution summary.
scope_guardrails: Do not train a separate classifier for significance.
Base the selection on scPSS-generated score, p-value, q-value, and condition outputs.

artifact_checks: significant_cells.csv contains cell identifiers and significance fields
evaluation.reference_strategy: Evaluate overlap with the reference significant-cell set derived from fixed q-value and score thresholds.

title: Integrated versus raw scoring comparison
prompt: Compare scPSS outputs before and after Harmony integration. Run raw scoring, run harmony_integrate plus scoring, and export a joined comparison table for the same cells.
scope_guardrails: Do not substitute another integration package or latent representation.
Keep the comparison centered on scPSS raw-versus-integrated outputs.
input_spec: One .h5ad file with a batch or sample column suitable for Harmony.

artifact_checks: integration_vs_raw_scores.csv contains both raw and integrated score columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare paired raw-versus-integrated score deltas against the reference run for the same cells.

title: Parameter sweep and best-config export
prompt: Sweep over scPSS parameter settings for distance metric and search ranges, record the resulting outlier ratios, and export the best-performing configuration according to a predefined objective.
scope_guardrails: Do not add model families or optimizers that are not already part of scPSS.
The selected best configuration must come from the evaluated sweep table.
input_spec: A parameter grid describing candidate settings to evaluate.

artifact_checks: parameter_sweep.csv contains one row per evaluated setting
evaluation.reference_strategy: Score exact match on the selected best configuration, with optional tolerance on numeric thresholds when multiple configs are near-tied.

title: Distance-metric comparison table
prompt: Run scPSS with multiple supported distance metrics on the same reference/query split and export a comparison table of score distributions and outlier ratios.
scope_guardrails: Do not introduce distance metrics outside the repo-supported set.
Keep the comparison table grounded in scPSS-produced outputs.
input_spec: A list of repo-supported distance metrics.

artifact_checks: distance_metric_comparison.csv contains one row per metric
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare metric ordering and reported outlier ratios against a canonical scPSS comparison run.

title: Condition-label cohort summary
prompt: Run scPSS and summarize the assigned condition labels across reference and query cohorts. Export a per-cell label table and an aggregated cohort summary.
scope_guardrails: Do not invent new condition labels outside scPSS outputs.
Use scPSS condition assignments as the primary signal.

artifact_checks: cell_conditions.csv contains cell identifiers and condition labels
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare cell-level labels and aggregated cohort counts against a reference scPSS run.

title: Reference-split sensitivity analysis
prompt: Evaluate how scPSS scores change under two fixed reference/query split definitions and export a matched-cell sensitivity table plus a short summary.
scope_guardrails: Do not redesign the split-analysis objective outside the given splits.
Keep the analysis centered on matched scPSS outputs.

artifact_checks: split_sensitivity_scores.csv contains matched cells across both splits
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare paired score changes against a reference two-split scPSS analysis.

title: Ranked query-cell report
prompt: Run scPSS and export a ranked report of query cells sorted by pathological shift score, including p-value, q-value, and condition columns.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace ranking with an unrelated model score.
Use scPSS-generated values only.

artifact_checks: ranked_query_cells.csv contains score, p-value, q-value, and condition columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Score overlap between participant and reference top-k ranked query cells.

title: Outlier-threshold comparison report
prompt: Apply multiple fixed score or q-value thresholds to scPSS outputs and export a threshold comparison table for pathological-cell counts.
scope_guardrails: Do not fit new threshold optimizers.
Use only the provided thresholds over scPSS outputs.

artifact_checks: threshold_comparison.csv contains one row per threshold
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare thresholded pathological-cell counts against the canonical reference table.

title: Reaction classification and naming table
prompt: For a reaction table, use Rxn-INSIGHT to parse each reaction and export standardized reaction info including reaction class and standardized reaction name.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace Rxn-INSIGHT classification with an external reaction classifier.
Use repo-native reaction parsing and naming outputs.
input_spec: A reaction table with reaction SMILES strings and bundled metadata fields.

artifact_checks: reaction_info.csv contains reaction identifier, class, and standardized name columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare class and standardized-name fields against a reference table produced with the same Rxn-INSIGHT version.

title: Reaction-center motif extraction
prompt: Use Rxn-INSIGHT to extract reaction-center information, functional groups, ring information, byproducts, and scaffold annotations for each reaction in a small table.
scope_guardrails: Do not define new motif schemas outside repo-native outputs.
Stay within reaction-center and structure annotations already exposed by Rxn-INSIGHT.
input_spec: A small reaction table with reaction SMILES strings.

artifact_checks: reaction_motifs.csv contains reaction-center and scaffold-related columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare exported motif fields against a reference annotation table and average exact-match rate across supported fields.

title: Reaction database creation and distribution summary
prompt: Create an Rxn-INSIGHT-compatible reaction database from a tabular reaction dataset, then summarize class and name distributions across the analyzed reactions.
scope_guardrails: Do not build a custom database schema unrelated to Rxn-INSIGHT.
Use the repo's analyzed database fields and distribution helpers.
input_spec: A reaction table with at least one reaction column and optional solvent, reagent, or catalyst metadata.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compute distribution distance against the canonical Rxn-INSIGHT analyzed database and derived counts.

title: Similar-reaction retrieval
prompt: Given one or more query reactions and a library of analyzed reactions, retrieve the nearest reaction precedents using Rxn-INSIGHT fingerprinting and similarity search.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace the retrieval stage with a separate embedding model or vector database.
Use Rxn-INSIGHT fingerprints and similarity ranking.
input_spec: A library table derived from the bundled 1000-reaction dataset or an analyzed database created from it.

artifact_checks: retrieval_results.csv contains query identifier, retrieved reaction identifier, and rank columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare ranked precedents against the canonical Rxn-INSIGHT top-k retrieval results.

title: Condition suggestion from precedents
prompt: Use Rxn-INSIGHT to suggest solvents, catalysts, and reagents for new reactions by retrieving similar reactions from a precedent library and aggregating ranked condition outputs.
scope_guardrails: Do not train a separate recommendation model.
Base suggestions on Rxn-INSIGHT precedent retrieval and ranking helpers.
input_spec: A reaction library with condition metadata.

artifact_checks: condition_suggestions.csv contains ranked solvent, catalyst, or reagent suggestions
evaluation.reference_strategy: Score ranked condition lists against the reference suggestions generated from the canonical precedent library.

title: Neighbor-overlap report for a query panel
prompt: For a fixed panel of query reactions, retrieve top-k neighbors and export an overlap report that compares shared precedents across the queries.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace Rxn-INSIGHT similarity search.
Keep the overlap report grounded in retrieved neighbors from the bundled library.
input_spec: A fixed panel of query reactions.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare shared-neighbor sets and overlap counts against a canonical retrieval run.

title: Scaffold cohort summary
prompt: Use Rxn-INSIGHT scaffold extraction to build a scaffold summary table for a reaction subset and report the most common scaffold patterns.
scope_guardrails: Do not define scaffold logic outside repo-native outputs.
Build the cohort summary from Rxn-INSIGHT annotations.
input_spec: A fixed subset of reactions from the bundled example table.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare scaffold annotations and top-scaffold frequencies against a reference summary.

title: Byproduct and ring-annotation summary
prompt: Extract byproduct and ring annotations for each reaction in a small bundled table and export an aggregated summary of the observed patterns.
scope_guardrails: Do not invent additional byproduct schemas.
Use repo-native ring and byproduct annotations only.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare row-level annotations and aggregated counts against a canonical Rxn-INSIGHT run.

title: Reaction-name frequency audit
prompt: Build a frequency table of standardized reaction names for a library subset and export the top names with counts.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace standardized names with custom aliases.
Use Rxn-INSIGHT name outputs as the audit target.
input_spec: A fixed subset of the bundled library.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare name-frequency counts against the canonical analyzed subset.

title: Class-specific condition ranking export
prompt: For a fixed query set, export ranked solvent, catalyst, and reagent suggestions and summarize the dominant condition choices by reaction class.
scope_guardrails: Do not introduce a custom class-conditioned recommender.
Use repo-native condition ranking outputs.
input_spec: A fixed query set and the bundled precedent library.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare ranked condition outputs and class-level summaries against the canonical suggestion pipeline.

title: Simulated star-field image generation
prompt: Using Photutils local bundled parameter tables, generate a synthetic star-field image suitable for downstream background estimation and source detection. Export the simulated image and the exact source parameter table used to create it.
scope_guardrails: Do not use an unrelated image simulator outside Photutils and Astropy models.
Keep the image-generation path grounded in the repo's datasets and model-image utilities.
input_spec: One bundled parameter table from photutils/datasets/data.
A declared output image shape and model configuration.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare the generated image and parameter table against a canonical seeded Photutils reference run.

title: 2D background and RMS estimation
prompt: Estimate the 2D background and background RMS of an astronomical image with Photutils and export the background maps plus a compact summary of the chosen settings.
scope_guardrails: Do not swap in a non-Photutils background estimator as the main workflow.
Keep the result centered on Background2D outputs and parameters.
input_spec: One 2D astronomical image in FITS form.

artifact_checks: background_map.fits and background_rms_map.fits are readable as FITS
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare both maps against a canonical Background2D run with fixed settings.

title: Threshold-based source detection
prompt: Detect astronomical sources in an image by estimating a threshold and running Photutils source detection. Export the segmentation image and a compact table of detected object centroids or labels.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace Photutils segmentation with a custom connected-components implementation.
Use repo-native thresholding and source-detection functions.
input_spec: One astronomical image, optionally with a background-subtracted version or mask.

artifact_checks: detected_sources.csv contains source labels or centroid columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Match detected sources to reference centroids using a fixed pixel tolerance.

title: DAOFIND star detection workflow
prompt: Run star detection with Photutils DAOStarFinder on a prepared image, tune the core kernel parameters, and export the detected star catalog together with the settings used.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace DAOStarFinder with an unrelated star finder.
Keep detection outputs and parameter tuning within the DAOStarFinder interface.
input_spec: A threshold and FWHM search space or fixed settings.

artifact_checks: dao_stars.csv contains centroid and flux-like DAOStarFinder output columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare detected stars against a canonical DAOStarFinder run, using fixed matching radius and thresholds.

title: Segmentation with deblending
prompt: Build a deblended segmentation map for a crowded astronomical image using Photutils SourceFinder or detect_sources plus deblend_sources, then export the final segmentation artifacts.
scope_guardrails: Do not implement an unrelated watershed or clustering pipeline outside Photutils.
The final segmentation must come from Photutils segmentation and deblending components.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Score overlap against a canonical deblended segmentation and exported segment catalog.

title: Aperture photometry for catalog sources
prompt: Measure source fluxes for catalog positions using Photutils aperture photometry, including local-background context or uncertainty handling when available. Export the photometry table and run summary.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace aperture photometry with a custom summation routine outside Photutils.
Use Photutils aperture objects and aperture_photometry as the main measurement path.
input_spec: Aperture definitions and optional error or mask arrays.

artifact_checks: aperture_photometry.csv contains source identifiers, positions, and measured flux columns
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare matched-source fluxes against a canonical Photutils aperture-photometry reference run.

title: Source catalog export from segmentation
prompt: Build a Photutils SourceCatalog from a segmentation result and export a compact source-property table for detected objects.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace SourceCatalog with a custom measurement table.
Use Photutils segmentation outputs as the catalog source.
input_spec: One astronomical image and a segmentation map.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare exported source properties against a canonical SourceCatalog run.

title: Aperture growth-curve comparison
prompt: Measure fluxes for a fixed source list with multiple circular aperture sizes and export a growth-curve table plus a compact summary.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace aperture photometry with a custom curve-of-growth implementation.
Use Photutils aperture objects and repeated aperture_photometry calls.

artifact_checks: growth_curve_photometry.csv contains one row per source-radius pair
evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare per-source growth curves against the canonical Photutils reference run.

title: Local-background aperture photometry
prompt: Perform local-background corrected aperture photometry for a fixed source list using circular apertures plus annulus-based background estimates.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace annulus-based background estimation with an unrelated method.
Use Photutils aperture primitives as the main workflow.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Compare corrected source fluxes against a canonical local-background photometry run.

title: Injected-source recovery analysis
prompt: Generate a synthetic star field from bundled parameter tables, run source detection, and export a recovery report comparing injected and recovered sources.
scope_guardrails: Do not replace the synthetic image generator or detector with external tools.
Keep the recovery analysis grounded in Photutils-generated inputs and outputs.
input_spec: A fixed image shape and detection settings.

evaluation.reference_strategy: Match recovered detections to injected source positions using a fixed tolerance.
